title: Acyclic category hierarchy with single-node transaction assignment
description: Choose the hierarchy representation, assignment target, and roll-up semantics after the owner rejected the flat category assumption.
decision: Represent the current hierarchy as each Spend Category having zero or one active parent, with append-only parent-change history. A transaction has zero or one active assignment to any active category node. Direct totals include assignments to that node; subtree totals include direct plus descendant assignments exactly once. Create, rename, reparent, archive, and reactivate validate sibling-name uniqueness, active ancestry, and acyclicity transactionally.
rationale: An adjacency relationship is the smallest durable model that supports owner-required hierarchy, stable IDs, cheap parent moves at personal scale, and deterministic roll-ups. Allowing assignment to any node avoids forced recategorization when the owner has only a broad classification, while exact and subtree modes prevent ambiguous totals.
